notes: These deliberately incomplete targets use expected_decision=block because current SkillGuard authority is mandatory.
Each case also preserves its intended static finding so the report explains both the local defect and the missing current authority.

fixtures.target_rule: An ungoverned target with a missing SKILL.md frontmatter description must block current single-skill analysis.
known_limitations: This fixture covers the missing-description finding inside the mandatory-current-authority block branch.

fixtures.target_rule: An ungoverned target with a missing declared SKILL.md reference must block current single-skill analysis.
known_limitations: This fixture covers the missing-reference finding inside the mandatory-current-authority block branch.

fixtures.target_rule: An ungoverned target with unsupported broad claim text must block current single-skill analysis.
known_limitations: This fixture covers the unsupported broad-claim finding inside the mandatory-current-authority block branch.

skipped_checks: These fixtures intentionally do not cover a current-authority target with only a static fail-class finding.
These fixtures do not replace the passing good_single_skill or good_suite fixtures.
residual_risk: The fixtures are local negative examples and do not prove broad checker behavior.
A separate current-authority fixture is required if isolated fail-class static analysis needs coverage.
claim_boundary: This static bad-fixture manifest covers only the listed negative fixture cases and current local files. It does not prove runtime checker execution, fixture coverage, CLI checks, tests, suite automation, package publication, code-contract validation, release readiness, or future AI behavior without separate current evidence.
